{"key_event":"Maastricht integration and 1995 strike veto","reasoning":"European integration, privatization, cohabitation, and decentralization reduced the French national state’s discretionary command, though security powers, fiscal discipline, and labor regulation kept overall administrative capacity very strong. Civil society remained exceptionally capable of constraining policy, as shown by the Maastricht backlash and 1995 strikes, but weak membership, unemployment, and exclusion slightly offset that strength.","society_change":-0.1,"society_power":8.9,"state_change":-0.2,"state_power":8.5}
